pattern: CEO fraud - urgent wire transfer request
description: Email impersonating CEO/executive requesting urgent wire transfer or gift card purchase
indicators: Requests wire transfer or gift cards
examples: I need you to process an urgent wire transfer. Keep this confidential.
Can you purchase some gift cards for a client? I'll reimburse you.
I'm in a meeting but need you to handle this payment immediately.

description: Email claiming to be from vendor requesting payment to new bank account
examples: Please update our banking details for future payments.
Our account has changed. Send the pending invoice payment to this new account.

description: Email claiming to be from IT requiring password or credential verification
examples: IT Department: Your password expires today. Click here to update.
Helpdesk: We need to verify your account. Please confirm your password.

description: Email claiming to be from HR requesting personal information or document review
examples: HR: Please review and sign the updated employee handbook.
Payroll: Verify your bank details for next month's salary deposit.

description: Fake job offer requesting personal information or upfront payment
indicators: Asks for upfront payment for equipment
examples: You've been selected for a remote position. Send your details to proceed.
Job offer: $5000/week working from home. Send SSN and bank details.

description: Unsolicited tech support offering to fix non-existent problems
examples: We detected issues with your computer. Let us connect remotely to fix it.
Your software license is invalid. Call us to resolve.

description: Email using artificial time pressure to force quick decisions
examples: This offer expires in 2 hours. Act now!

description: Email using threats or fear to compel action
examples: Legal notice: Pay immediately or face prosecution.
We have evidence of your activities. Pay to prevent exposure.
